pi_items.source: MLB.com official field dimensions - bases are 90 feet apart
MLB.com official specification - pitcher's rubber front edge is 60 feet 6 inches (60.5 feet) from home plate apex

MLB.com official specification - distance from home plate to second base is 127 feet 3 3/8 inches (127.28125 feet). Task value (127.279) appears to be mathematical diagonal (90*sqrt(2)≈127.2792), which differs from official spec by ~0.027 inches.
MLB.com Base Sizes rule change effective 2023 season - bases increased from 15-inch to 18-inch squares to reduce collision injuries
MLB.com official field dimensions and multiple sources (Turface Athletics, UmpireBible)
MLB.com official specification - pitcher's rubber front edge positioned 60 feet 6 inches from home plate apex
Baseball geometry - foul lines extend from home plate at 45-degree angles forming a right angle (90°) with each other
MLB.com and multiple sources confirm home plate is a 5-sided pentagon (introduced in 1900)

privileged_info_summary: The baseball infield task specifies official MLB dimensions for bases (90 feet apart), pitcher's mound (60 feet 6 inches to front edge), and home plate (pentagon). Most geometric specifications are verified against official MLB rules. However, two discrepancies exist: (1) The base size is specified as 15-inch squares, but MLB changed this to 18-inch squares in 2023. (2) The second base distance is given as 127.279 feet (the mathematical diagonal of 90*sqrt(2)), while the official MLB specification is 127 feet 3 3/8 inches (127.28125 feet)—a difference of approximately 0.027 inches. The task involves creating synthetic data (a CAD drawing) based on real baseball field specifications.
